title: Monument Valley🇺🇸 #drone #usa #monumentvalley #navajonation #mavicair2s
creator: _elisacappelletti_ (Instagram)
notes: SUBGENRE-DIVERSIFYING EXEMPLAR (desert/canyon/mesa landscapes) -- not the pack's original single worked example (that role is bbc-earth-aerial.json, per spec AC3.4), but built to the exact same rigor/honesty standard. Independently re-verified this session via WebFetch of the live Instagram reel page (https://www.instagram.com/reel/DIQR5ZDAd9P/), which returned: creator/username '_elisacappelletti_'; post date 'April 9, 2025'; caption 'Monument Valley🇺🇸'; hashtags #drone #usa #monumentvalley #navajonation #mavicair2s; tagged accounts @djiglobal @dji @dji_de; post type 'Reel (video)'; and explicitly 'No CC licensing marking visible on this post.' This matches the candidate record handed to me field-for-field (same creator handle, same caption text, same hashtag set, same April 9 2025 date), so I am treating it as independently confirmed rather than merely trusting the prior agent's JSON verbatim -- this WebFetch call is this session's own primary-source verification, not a restatement of the earlier agent's claim. A follow-up WebSearch this session ('_elisacappelletti_ Instagram drone Monument Valley Mavic Air 2S') returned no results directly attributable to this creator or reel (only generic Monument Valley/Mavic Air 2S forum and news pages), so no independent secondary-source corroboration of the creator's profile or reach was found. A further WebFetch of the creator's profile page (https://www.instagram.com/_elisacappelletti_/) returned only base64-encoded profile-header images with no extractable bio, follower count, or professional-credential text -- so no follower-count/engagement signal could be obtained in this session; this is disclosed rather than papered over with an invented number. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved' because the WebFetch explicitly reported no Creative Commons marking on the post (per this project's stated default: assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise) -- consequently `local_media_path` stays null and no video/image file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. `duration` is left null: Instagram's reel page did not surface an exact runtime in the WebFetch response and no oEmbed-equivalent endpoint was queried (Instagram's oEmbed API requires app-review access this session does not have), so rather than guess a plausible reel length (Reels are capped well under 90s but the exact cut point is unverified), this field stays unset. `award_or_showcase_provenance` is left null: this is an individual creator's personal travel/drone reel with no juried contest, broadcaster, or festival attribution found anywhere in this session's research -- unlike this pack's contest-winner and broadcaster exemplars, inventing a showcase provenance here would be fabrication. `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. These are calibrated deliberately lower than this pack's contest-winning/broadcaster exemplars (which cluster 0.777-0.817): the DJI Mavic Air 2S (confirmed via the #mavicair2s hashtag) is a genuine 1-inch-sensor, 5.4K-capable consumer/prosumer drone with 3-axis gimbal stabilization -- a real, disclosed technical baseline above entry-level hardware, supporting sharpness=0.74 and motion_smoothness=0.72 above a low floor -- but this is an unvetted, un-juried personal Reel with no verifiable engagement/follower signal, no stated resolution/export settings, and no evidence of color grading or professional post-production, so all three signals sit at the low end of the 0.72-0.88 band rather than matching the professionally-produced/award-vetted exemplars already in the pack. `composite_score` IS a real computation, not hand-computed: produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.74, exposure=0.73, motion_smoothness=0.72, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` (no explicit version argument -- resolved to the module's current default, weights_version 'default-v2': 0.25 each across sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness/composition) via `.venv/bin/python3.10`, returning composite_score=0.73 exactly. Because `composition` is `None`, `compute_composite_score` skips the composition term entirely (its weight-or-score-is-None guard), so the result reduces to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals -- identical to what the legacy 'default-v1' profile (1/3 each, composition weight fixed at 0.0) would have produced for the same three inputs; both were checked in this session and agree exactly. Editorial note (WebSearch side-finding, disclosed for completeness, not scored): search results surfaced that Monument Valley/Navajo Nation land is commonly reported as off-limits to recreational drone flights absent tribal permission -- this is a third-party claim from forum/news sources, not independently confirmed against Navajo Nation policy in this session, and has no bearing on this record's license_category or scores, but is noted here in the interest of not silently omitting a potentially relevant fact surfaced during research.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: format='short-form-reel' is grounded in this session's own WebFetch of the reel page, which explicitly returned 'Post Type: Reel (video)' -- a structural/platform fact (Instagram Reels are a capped-duration short-form format by definition), not a playback-derived judgment. No live playback or scrubbing of the video was attempted in this session: browser automation (claude-in-chrome) tools are available in this environment but were not invoked, since exercising them would require an interactive browser-selection step (per that tool's own instructions) that is out of scope for this bounded verification task. Consequently estimated_cut_count, avg_shot_length_seconds, and transition_styles_observed are left null/empty rather than guessed -- WebFetch of the reel page surfaced only the caption, hashtags, and tagged accounts already covered above, with no text describing cut rhythm, shot count, transition style, music, or pacing, and the earlier WebSearch attempt for creator+reel-specific commentary returned only unrelated generic Monument Valley drone-footage results. No genuine text-provenance or playback basis exists for those three fields, so per the schema's honesty contract they stay unset.
